fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Landscaped Area; Front Driveway; Pedestrian Walkways
fr.recommendations: Widening or enhancing the visibility of the single access point may facilitate quicker and more efficient responder entry in case of an emergency situation. Managing and possibly reducing the dense vegetation around access routes and near the main building could improve visibility and navigational ease for emergency personnel. Additionally, ensuring that pedestrian walkways are clear and well-marked might support effective movement and coordination during response operations. Enhanced clarity and accessibility around the front driveway could further aid in positioning emergency vehicles efficiently, facilitating staging and rapid deployment of resources.

wildfire.conditions: Scattered Trees; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Defensible Space; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Green Space
wildfire.recommendations: To improve property preparedness and reduce wildfire exposure, consider maintaining an effective defensible space by managing the vegetation immediately adjacent to the structure. Ensuring adequate separation between the structure and surrounding vegetation, particularly where the vegetated perimeter meets the building, could reduce fuel continuity. Trimming or selectively removing certain trees and underbrush in proximity to the home may enhance defensible space without compromising the site’s natural aesthetics. Maintaining and possibly expanding the existing open green spaces around the property would further support fuel breaks, thus minimizing opportunities for fire spread close to key structures. The ongoing management of these green spaces, combined with a strategic approach to fuel reduction, can foster long-term wildfire resilience while supporting potential firefighter access and suppression operations if needed.
